title: ChartMogul vs Baremetrics vs Lightweight Stripe Trackers
meta_description: ChartMogul and Baremetrics are overkill for most solo SaaS founders under $50k MRR. Here's what you actually need to track Stripe revenue.
h1: ChartMogul vs Baremetrics vs Lightweight Stripe Trackers: What Solo Founders Under $50k MRR Actually Need
body_markdown:
You opened Stripe this morning to check if that annual plan renewed. Then you clicked into Payments. Then Customers. Then back to the dashboard. Five minutes later you still don't know what your MRR actually is, because Stripe's built-in MRR number includes a failed payment from last Tuesday and a $2,400 one-time invoice you sent a contractor.

So you start Googling. ChartMogul? $129/mo. Baremetrics? $129/mo. You're doing $8,400 MRR. That's 1.5% of your revenue going to a dashboard that tells you what your revenue is.

This is the annoying middle ground a lot of solo SaaS founders sit in: Stripe's native reporting isn't enough, but the category leaders are priced for Series A companies with a head of finance. Let's break down what each tier actually gives you, and when it makes sense to pay for what.

## The three tiers of Stripe revenue tools

The market has quietly split into three layers:

1. **Full analytics suites** (ChartMogul, Baremetrics, ProfitWell/Paddle) — $100-$500+/mo
2. **Lightweight Stripe trackers** — $0-$20/mo
3. **DIY** — spreadsheets, Stripe Sigma, custom scripts

Most advice online assumes you're picking between tier 1 options. If you're under $50k MRR and it's just you (or you and a cofounder), that framing is wrong. The real question is whether you need tier 1 at all yet.

## ChartMogul: the thorough one

ChartMogul is what you graduate to when you have an investor who asks questions like "what's your net revenue retention cohort by acquisition channel?"

### What it does well

- **Subscription analytics depth.** Cohort retention, LTV by plan, expansion vs contraction MRR, segmentation by any customer attribute you pipe in.
- **Data cleanup.** It handles the weird stuff — proration, plan changes mid-cycle, paused subscriptions, currency conversion — without you having to think about it.
- **Integrations.** HubSpot, Salesforce, Intercom, Slack. If your GTM motion involves a CRM, this matters.
- **Multi-source billing.** If you have Stripe + Chargebee + manual invoices, it consolidates them.

### What it costs

The free tier technically exists up to $10k MRR, but it's heavily limited. Paid plans start at $129/mo and climb quickly with MRR. At $30k MRR you're looking at $200+/mo. At $100k MRR, closer to $500.

### When it makes sense

You've raised a round. You have a board. You're running paid acquisition at scale and need to tie CAC back to cohorts. You have more than one pricing page and more than one billing system.

If none of that is true, you're paying for a dashboard nobody opens twice a week.

## Baremetrics: the pretty one

Baremetrics is ChartMogul's friendlier sibling. Same rough feature set, different UX priorities.

### What it does well

- **Faster onboarding.** Connect Stripe, wait an hour, everything is there.
- **Public dashboards.** The "Open Startups" trend — founders sharing real MRR publicly — was largely a Baremetrics feature. Still useful for transparency-driven marketing.
- **Cancellation Insights and Recover.** Built-in dunning and churn survey tools. These are real products, not just dashboards.
- **Forecasting.** Decent MRR projections based on current trajectory.

### What it costs

Starts at $129/mo for the Metrics plan. Recover and Cancellation Insights are add-ons that push the total north of $200/mo fast. No meaningful free tier anymore.

### When it makes sense

You want the dunning + analytics combo in one tool. You care about the polished UX and are going to actually stare at the charts regularly. You're planning to do public transparency as a marketing play.

### Where it falls short for small founders

At $8k MRR, $129/mo is 1.6% of your revenue. At $20k MRR it's still 0.65%. For a metric — your own revenue — that you can calculate with a reasonably short SQL query on Stripe Sigma for $0.02.

## Lightweight Stripe trackers: the third option

A new category has shown up over the last couple of years: tools that do one specific thing — show you your real MRR, ARR, active subscriber count, and churn — without the analytics-suite pricing or complexity.

They're usually built by solo developers who hit this exact problem themselves. They tend to share a few traits:

- Flat or very cheap pricing ($0-$20/mo, not MRR-indexed)
- Read-only Stripe API connection, usually set up in under 5 minutes
- A single dashboard or mobile-friendly page, not a 40-report suite
- No CRM integrations, no cohort analysis, no forecasting models

### What you lose

You lose a lot, honestly. No LTV by segment. No NRR cohorts. No Slack alerts tied to customer attributes. No forecasting. If any of that matters to you right now, stop reading and go buy ChartMogul.

### What you keep

- Real MRR, calculated correctly (excluding one-off invoices, handling annual plans as monthly, etc.)
- Active subscriber count
- Churn rate
- New vs lost MRR this month
- Usually a mobile view so you can check it without opening your laptop

For a solo founder under $50k MRR, that list covers roughly 95% of the numbers you actually check on a given week.

## The DIY route

Worth mentioning because it's legitimate: Stripe Sigma lets you run SQL against your Stripe data for pennies. You can write an MRR query once, save it, and open it whenever.

> The catch is that "write an MRR query once" hides an enormous amount of edge-case complexity. Proration, plan changes, annual amortization, free trials converting mid-month, failed payments in retry — getting this right takes a weekend, and keeping it right takes ongoing attention.

If you enjoy SQL and have a Sunday to burn, it's a real option. If you don't, the $10-$20/mo a lightweight tracker charges is effectively you paying someone else to have already solved these edge cases.

## A decision framework

Here's a rough heuristic based on where most solo founders actually land:

**Under $10k MRR:** Stripe dashboard + a lightweight tracker, or just Sigma if you're technical. Anything more is premature optimization. You don't have enough data for cohort analysis to be statistically meaningful anyway.

**$10k-$50k MRR, solo or two people:** Lightweight tracker. You need a clean MRR number you can trust at a glance, churn visibility, and a mobile view. You do not need a CRM-integrated analytics suite. The $100+/mo you'd spend on Baremetrics is better spent on one more hour of a contractor's time or a year of a landing page tool.

**$50k-$200k MRR, small team:** Start evaluating Baremetrics or ChartMogul seriously, especially if you're hiring, raising, or running meaningful paid acquisition. The depth starts to pay for itself.

**$200k+ MRR or post-Series A:** ChartMogul, usually. The integration surface area and segmentation depth matter at that scale.

## The honest part

Most content on this topic is written by affiliates of the big tools, which is why the default recommendation is always ChartMogul or Baremetrics. They're both good products. They're also priced assuming you're a funded company where $150/mo is a rounding error.

If you're bootstrapped at $12k MRR, $150/mo is not a rounding error. It's a meaningful percentage of your product's entire cost base. Spending it on analytics you could replace with a $9/mo tool and a Sigma query means you're optimizing the wrong thing.

This is roughly why we built [Stripe Revenue Tracker](#) — we were two-person SaaS founders staring at the same $129/mo invoice and deciding we'd rather have a clean MRR number on our phone for less than the cost of a coffee. It doesn't do cohort analysis. It won't integrate with your CRM. It tells you what you actually make, without you logging into Stripe. For most people reading this, that's the whole job.

## TL;DR

- ChartMogul and Baremetrics are excellent products aimed at companies with budgets that don't feel $130/mo.
- Under $50k MRR, you almost certainly don't use 80% of what they offer.
- A lightweight Stripe tracker gives you the 20% you actually check, for 10% of the cost.
- Revisit the decision at $50k MRR, or when you hire someone whose job title includes "growth" or "finance."

The best analytics tool is the one you actually open. For a lot of solo founders, that's not a 40-chart suite — it's a single clean number.
cover_image_prompt: Editorial illustration, dark mode, minimalist concept art style. Three abstract geometric dashboard shapes floating at different scales against a deep charcoal background: one large, dense, complex polygonal structure on the left suggesting an over-engineered control panel; one medium-sized mirrored shape in the center; and one small, elegant single circular gauge on the right, glowing softly. Subtle accent colors in muted teal and dusty orange, thin line work, soft ambient glow. Sense of scale and comparison. Premium tech magazine aesthetic, restrained, intellectual mood.
